mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation through a lower discount rate and reduced recession probability, so broad index returns should be positive over the next month when policy is easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the policy rate is high, equity duration becomes more sensitive to discount-rate pressure and credit tightening, so elevated FEDFUNDS predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the effect weakens to near zero after controlling for recent CPIAUCSL changes.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate is a direct early warning of weakening household income and demand, so month-over-month increases in UNRATE should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if a 0.3pp or larger 3-month rise in UNRATE is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in most instances, or if the sign flips during non-recessionary periods without inflation pressure.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized pickup in retail sales and industrial production reflects broad real-demand acceleration, which should lift corporate revenue expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive momentum in RSAFS and INDPRO does not outperform either series alone in forecasting 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal has no positive edge over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a forward indicator of cyclical demand, construction employment, and credit appetite; when HOUST accelerates from a depressed base, equity returns should turn positive over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST gains above 5% are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the signal is eliminated after excluding months with large FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production indicates stronger real output and improving operating leverage for cyclical firms, so positive month-over-month INDPRO momentum should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum fails to correspond to positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the relationship is not statistically distinguishable from zero in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in payroll growth and retail sales indicates weakening final demand and deteriorating earnings visibility, so the joint condition should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth < 0.1% and RSAFS growth < 0% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined weak-payrolls/weak-sales trigger does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if either proxy alone explains the signal equally well.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflation regime, a year-over-year slowdown in CPI combined with a stable policy rate lowers real-rate pressure and supports equity multiples, so the pair should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12m inflation is falling while FEDFUNDS is unchanged for 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if falling CPIAUCSL inflation plus a flat FEDFUNDS rate does not coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power beyond CPIAUCSL alone.
